title: fix: harden Vast Flux deployment and canary checks
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
- Runs a pre-created, remotely managed Cloudflare Tunnel from a scoped token file instead of copying an account certificate onto Vast hosts.
- Persists shell-escaped Flux settings, enables high-performance Hugging Face downloads, and ignores the secret-bearing `.env.flux` file.
- Adds a deterministic production-path canary that compares direct Vast output with `gen.pollinations.ai` output so Fireworks cannot mask a broken route.
- Updates the Flux runbook with the active RTX 5090, current listed rate, and canary-before-cutover procedure.

Checks: `bash -n` on both scripts, validation failure-path tests, secret-pattern scan, `git diff --check`.

gist.headline: The canary gets a compass
gist.blurb: Vast Flux deployments now carry fewer secrets and more receipts. A deterministic canary checks the actual production route before the hive points traffic at it.
gist.summary: Hardened the Vast-hosted Flux deployment with scoped Cloudflare Tunnel tokens, persisted shell-escaped settings, accelerated Hugging Face downloads, and a new canary that compares direct Vast output against gen.pollinations.ai before cutover.
gist.impact: Image generation routing gains a repeatable pre-cutover check that verifies the Vast Flux path itself, rather than allowing a Fireworks fallback to hide a broken deployment.
gist.visual_concept: Depict Polly as a careful deployment gardener using a tiny compass and checklist beside two cozy image-output terminals: one labeled Vast and one labeled gen.pollinations.ai, both showing matching pixel flowers. A small friendly canary bird and a protected tunnel pipe symbolize deterministic route verification and scoped secret handling, with code-plants and an RTX 5090 server nook grounding the scene.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly, a cute pixel art bee mascot with a yellow body, dark brown horizontal stripes, small black legs, two black antennae, black vertical oval eyes, bright pink rectangular blush marks, a small happy smile, and small rounded light blue translucent wings, wearing a tiny engineer tool belt as she compares matching pixel-flower images on two retro terminals labeled “VAST” and “GEN”; a friendly little canary holds a checklist beside a safely sealed tunnel pipe, with code-plants growing around a cozy RTX 5090 server shelf. Cozy 8-bit pixel art aesthetic, soft lime green (#ecf874) and pastel gradient background, chunky retro sprites, warm lighting, lo-fi vibes like Stardew Valley or A Short Hike, nostalgic but beautiful.
